body: PT Bank Jago Tbk's shares increased after completing the Management Employee Stock Option Plan Phase I, with an exercise price of IDR 2,150 and a mesop conversion value of IDR 9.28 billion, resulting in a total of 13.86 billion shares. The company's shares were recorded at IDR 2,580, fluctuating in the green zone, with a controlling composition of 41.48% controlling shareholders and 58.52% non-controlling shareholders.
title: Bank Jago's shares rise after completing management employee stock option plan, with 4.31 million shares exercised.

body: PT Bangun Kosambi Sukses Tbk (CBDK) has acquired 135.04 series B shares of PT Industri Pameran Nusantara (IPN) worth IDR 2.3 trillion, gaining 99.99% control of IPN's shares, with funds to be used for constructing buildings and financing meetings, incentives, conferences, and exhibitions. The transaction, valued at IDR 2.3 trillion, exceeds 20% of CBDK's equity of IDR 8.14 trillion as of June 2024, and is classified as a material and affiliated transaction.
title: PT Bangun Kosambi Sukses acquires PT Industri Pameran Nusantara with 2.3 trillion rupiah deal.

body: PT Saraswanti Anugerah Makmur Tbk (SAMF) shares surged 37.3% to IDR 1,030 from IDR 750 after its stock split plan was approved last week, with the stock split ratio set at 1:2 and nominal value per share reduced to IDR 50 from IDR 100. The company's authorised capital of IDR 1.74 trillion will be divided into 34.8 billion shares with a total issued and fully paid-up capital of IDR 512.5 billion or 10.25 billion shares.
title: PT Saraswanti Anugerah Makmur's shares surge 37.3% after stock split plan approval.

body: Jackson Wijaya Limantara, son of Oei Tjie Goan, has received 1.86 billion controlling shares of PT Pabrik Kertas Tjiwi Kimia TBK (TKIM) from Teguh Ganda Wijaya, increasing his indirect ownership to 59.67% through ultimate controlling shareholders. This transaction, categorized as a stock gift related to leadership succession, has changed the ownership structure of TKIM, with Limantara now holding the majority stake.
title: Jackson Wijaya Limantara receives controlling shares of PT Pabrik Kertas Tjiwi Kimia TBK from his father Teguh Ganda Wijaya.

body: Wahyu Dwi Jatmiko, an individual investor, increased his stake in PT Logisticsplus International Tbk (LOGI) to 66.82% by acquiring 304 million shares, while Lee Suharlie purchased 42.05 million shares of PT Megapower Makmur Tbk (MGPK) to hold a 5.15% stake. Meanwhile, PT MNC Asia Holding Tbk (BHIT) executed the largest divestment, selling 53.49 million shares of PT MNC Land Tbk (KPIG), and other notable transactions involved PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI), PT Victoria Investama Tbk (VIVA), and PT Bank Amar Indonesia Tbk (BAB).
title: Multiple investors and companies make significant changes to their stakes in various Indonesian companies, including logistics, transportation, and financial services firms.

body: The merger between PT XL Axiata Tbk and PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk has sparked negative sentiment in the market, which is expected to pressure EXCL's financial performance, despite its positive growth with IDR 25.36 trillion in revenue and IDR 1.32 trillion in net profit in the first nine months of 2024. Meanwhile, tower companies like PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk and PT Dayamitra Telekomunikasi Tbk remain attractive investment opportunities due to their stable fundamentals and promising growth potential, with TOWR's recent acquisition expected to strengthen its net income.
title: XL Axiata and Smartfren Merger Expected to Pressure Financial Performance of Telecommunications Tower Companies

body: Foreign investors sold a net 1.04 billion shares in the Indonesian market on Monday, with PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO) experiencing the largest net sell of 1.22 billion shares, followed by PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) and PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA). Meanwhile, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) saw the largest net buy of 85.37 million shares, followed by PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk and PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk.
title: Foreign Investors Sell 1.04 Billion Shares in Indonesian Market, Led by Goto Gojek Tokopedia and Bank Central Asia

body: PT Xolare RCR Energy Tbk (SOLA) has secured a IDR 416.97 billion asphalt chip sealing project contract in West Kutai from PT Trubaindo Coal Mining through its subsidiary, PT Aplikasi Bitumen Indonesia, which is expected to boost the company's revenue. In Q3 2024, SOLA's revenue reached IDR 72.05 billion, with asphalt and construction contributing IDR 27.76 billion and IDR 44.29 billion, respectively, marking a significant increase from IDR 46.51 billion in the same period in 2023.
title: PT Xolare RCR Energy Secures IDR 416.97 Billion Asphalt Chip Sealing Project Contract in West Kutai.

body: PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (BUKA) has objected to a petition filed by PT Harmas Jalesveva to suspend debt payment obligations, citing that the petition is incorrect and based on a civil dispute that falls under the jurisdiction of the South Jakarta District Court. Bukalapak.com believes it is not a qualified debtor with overdue debts, and remains optimistic that the legal process will proceed fairly and objectively, with plans to take further legal actions to settle the issue professionally.
title: Bukalapak objects to debt payment suspension petition filed by Harmas Jalesveva, citing incorrect jurisdiction and disputing debt claims.

body: PT Mutuagung Lestari Tbk (MUTU) has purchased 30% of the emissions produced in the first international carbon exchange trading, supporting Indonesia's goal of achieving net zero emissions by 2060. As a validation and verification body for greenhouse gas emissions, MUTU ensures the accuracy of carbon emission data, generating trust among market players and serving as the foundation for a sustainable carbon market.
title: Mutu International participates in Indonesia's first international carbon trading, supporting the country's goal of achieving net zero emissions by 2060.

body: PT Media Retail Indonesia acquired a 5.85% stake in PT Era Media Sejahtera Tbk (ERAS) by buying 452.26 million shares, while Central Business Alliance Pte Ltd purchased 118.93 million shares of PT Communication Cable System Indonesia Tbk (CCSI), gaining a 9.02% ownership stake. Meanwhile, other investors, including DBS Bank Ltd and individual investors, made notable share purchases and sales in various companies, including PT Sarimelati Kencana Tbk (SRTK), PT Janu Putra Sejahtera Tbk (JPSS), and PT Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI).
title: Multiple companies in Indonesia experience changes in share ownership with new investors entering and existing ones adjusting their stakes.

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange has officially launched international-scale carbon trading, with a total transaction value of IDR 19.72 billion (approximately USD 1.3 million) from January to December 2024, and a trading volume of 413,764 tons CO2e. In December 2024, the carbon trading volume reached 1,578 tons CO2e, worth IDR 88.34 million, with an average daily transaction value of IDR 4.64 million.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Launches International-Scale Carbon Trading to Attract Global Investors

body: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) and World Bank have projected Indonesia's economic growth to be 5.1% in 2025, lower than the government's expectations, citing weak household consumption, particularly among lower-middle-income groups. The global economy is expected to remain stable at 3.3% according to the IMF, and 2.7% according to the World Bank, with the Indonesian government remaining optimistic despite the lowered forecasts.
title: IMF and World Bank Lower Indonesia's Economic Growth Forecast to 5.1 Percent

body: Investment realisation in Indonesia is expected to grow 8.83% in Q1 2025, albeit at a slower pace compared to Q4 2024's 9.59% growth, driven by sectors such as agriculture, wholesale retail trade, and transportation-warehousing. Meanwhile, 24.79% of respondents plan to invest in the first half of 2025, citing obstacles such as licensing, taxation, and interest rates.
title: Jakarta's Investment Realisation in Q1 2025 to Grow at Slower Pace Compared to Q4 2024
